title: REST API vs GraphQL: Choosing the Right Architecture
subTitle: Understanding modern API communication
shortDescription: A practical comparison of REST APIs and GraphQL, explaining when developers should choose each architecture in modern applications.
content: APIs act as the backbone of modern web applications. REST has long been the industry standard for building scalable web services. However, as applications became more complex, developers began facing issues such as over-fetching and under-fetching of data. GraphQL was introduced to solve these problems by allowing clients to request exactly the data they need.
contentTwo: While REST APIs are simple and easy to cache, GraphQL offers more flexibility in data fetching. However, GraphQL requires careful schema design and security implementation.

quote.content: Technology choices should be driven by system requirements, not trends.
quote.author: Ankit Singh

postList.title: REST APIs
postList.content: Standard HTTP based architecture using GET, POST, PUT and DELETE.

postList.title: GraphQL
postList.content: Flexible query-based API architecture introduced by Facebook.

postList.title: gRPC
postList.content: High-performance RPC framework used for microservices.

postList.title: WebSockets
postList.content: Real-time communication protocol used for live applications.

discount.title: Popular API Architectures
discount.subTitle: Common API styles used in modern systems:

contentThree: Developers should evaluate scalability, security, and system complexity before choosing an API architecture.

title: How Node.js Handles Concurrency
subTitle: Understanding the event loop
shortDescription: A deep dive into Node.js event loop architecture and how it enables non-blocking I/O operations.
content: Node.js is known for its non-blocking architecture powered by the event loop. Instead of creating new threads for every request, Node.js uses asynchronous callbacks and a single-threaded event loop to handle multiple requests efficiently.
contentTwo: Applications such as APIs, chat apps, and streaming platforms benefit greatly from Node.js because it can handle thousands of concurrent connections.

quote.content: Node.js changed backend development by making JavaScript a server-side language.
quote.author: Ankit Singh

postList.title: Event Loop
postList.content: Core component that processes async operations.

postList.title: Callbacks
postList.content: Functions executed after async operations complete.

postList.title: Async/Await
postList.content: Modern way to handle asynchronous operations.

contentThree: Understanding Node.js internals helps developers design scalable backend systems.

title: MongoDB Indexing Strategies
shortDescription: How indexing improves MongoDB query performance and reduces database latency.
content: Indexes in MongoDB allow databases to locate documents quickly without scanning entire collections.
contentTwo: MongoDB supports several index types including compound indexes, text indexes, and hashed indexes.

quote.content: Without indexes, databases become slow at scale.
quote.author: Ankit Singh

contentThree: Proper index design significantly improves performance in production environments.

title: Git Workflow for Professional Teams
shortDescription: A guide to Git workflows used by professional development teams.
content: Git workflows help development teams collaborate efficiently while maintaining clean code history.
contentTwo: Common workflows include Git Flow, GitHub Flow, and trunk-based development.

quote.content: Version control is the backbone of collaborative development.
quote.author: Ankit Singh

contentThree: A well-defined Git workflow ensures efficient collaboration and stable releases.

title: Microservices vs Monolith Architecture
shortDescription: Comparing monolithic and microservices architecture in modern software systems.
content: Software architecture decisions impact scalability, maintainability, and team productivity.
contentTwo: Microservices are beneficial when systems grow large and require independent scaling.

quote.content: Architecture decisions shape the long-term success of software.
quote.author: Ankit Singh

contentThree: Both architectures have advantages depending on system complexity and team size.

title: Docker for Developers: Containerizing Applications
subTitle: A beginner friendly introduction
shortDescription: Learn how Docker helps developers package applications and dependencies into portable containers.
content: Docker has transformed how applications are deployed by packaging code and dependencies into containers.
contentTwo: Containers ensure consistent environments across development, testing, and production.

quote.content: Containers solved the classic ‘it works on my machine’ problem.
quote.author: Ankit Singh

contentThree: Docker enables faster deployments and reliable infrastructure across environments.
